cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the correct order of the places from east to west. Let me start by reading the passage again and see what it says about the locations. The first part of the passage says: "Suppose that we lined up our roughly 14 million United States businesses in order of size, starting with the smallest, along an imaginary road from San Francisco to New York." So the road is from San Francisco to New York. That would mean San Francisco is on the west (since the US is oriented with west on the left if you're facing east). So San Francisco is the starting point, and New York is the eastern end. Then it says, "From San Francisco to about Reno, it is almost unnoticeable..." So Reno is between San Francisco and New York. Then, "From Reno eastward the poles increase in height until, near Columbus, Ohio -
